perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that it will hold in strict confidence and not disclose to any third party Confidential Information of the other, except as approved in writing by the other party to this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Company is permitted to disclose Confidential Information of Adept to personnel of Omron Corporation and Omron Management Center of America, Inc., provided that Company ensures compliance by such companies and persons with the terms and conditions hereof and Company remains responsible for any non-compliance. Each party will use the Confidential Information for no purpose other than for the Purpose. Each party will only disclose such information based on the Purpose to its professional advisors, employees and independent contractors who have a “need to know” such information, who are informed that the information is confidential and who are under obligations of confidentiality at least as stringent as those set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that it will hold in confidence and not disclose to any third party Confidential Information of the other, except as approved. Each party will use the Confidential Information for the Purpose. Each party will only disclose such information based on the Purpose to its professional advisors, employees and independent contractors.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'in strict confidence', 'in writing by the other party to this agreement', 'Company is permitted to disclose Confidential Information of Adept to personnel of Omron Corporation and Omron Management Center of America, Inc., provided that Company ensures compliance by such companies and persons with the terms and conditions hereof and Company remains responsible for any non-compliance.', 'who have a “need to know” such information, who are informed that the information is confidential and who are under obligations of confidentiality at least as stringent as those set forth herein.' weakens the confidentiality obligations. This removal weakens the explicit requirements that Omron must make sure it's employees are in compliance with the requirements and that each professional advisors, employees, and independent contractors are informed that the information is confidential and who are under obligations of confidentiality at least as stringent as those set forth herein. It introduces uncertainty about the standard of care required and who is responsible when this standard isn't met, therefore contradicting Section 8.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party recognizes that the disclosure of the other party’s Confidential Information may give rise to irreparable injury and acknowledges that remedies other than injunctive relief will not be adequate. Accordingly, each party shall have the right to seek equitable and injunctive relief (without the need to post a bond or other security) to prevent the unauthorized disclosure of its Confidential Information, as well as such damages or other relief as may be available in law or equity for any unauthorized use or disclosure of such information.
perturbation.justification: The original text placed strict obligations on maintaining confidentiality, including specific requirements for third-party disclosures and ensuring those parties adhered to stringent confidentiality terms. The changed text significantly weakens these obligations by removing 'in strict confidence', the need for written approval for third-party disclosure, the specific allowance for disclosing confidential information to Omron, and the requirement that professional advisors, employees, and independent contractors be subject to confidentiality obligations at least as stringent as those in the agreement. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which grants each party the right to seek injunctive relief to prevent unauthorized disclosure. The weakened confidentiality obligations in the changed text make it less clear what constitutes an 'unauthorized disclosure', thus undercutting the basis for seeking injunctive relief and creating a strong, direct conflict within the contract.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the written request of the other party, each party shall promptly return to the other (or destroy if destruction is requested and provide written certification of destruction) all documents and other tangible and intangible materials representing the other’s Confidential Information and all copies thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the request of the other party, each party shall return to the other all documents and other tangible and intangible materials representing the other’s Confidential Information and all copies thereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'written', 'promptly', '(or destroy if destruction is requested and provide written certification of destruction)', the text removes critical methods on how to request and return/destroy Confidential Information. Leaving it open to interpretation on whether the requests needs to be written, how fast it should be returned and no mentioning on destruction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall continue in full force and effect for so long as the parties continue to exchange Confidential Information. This Agreement may be terminated by either party at any time upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other party, however its terms shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years from the date of such termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for 'written request', the specification of 'promptly' returning the information, and the option to destroy the information with written certification. The contradicted text states the agreement may be terminated with a 'thirty (30) days written notice'. Since the changed text removes the requirement of written request which creates an omission per the provided definition for this perturbation type, it directly contradicts the contradicted text requiring written notice, making it unclear if written requests or notices are still valid. This creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall not be reproduced in any form except as reasonably required for the Purpose. Any reproduction of any Confidential Information of the other party by either party shall remain the property of the disclosing party and shall contain any and all confidential or proprietary notices or legends which appear on the original, unless otherwise authorized in writing by the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information shall not be reproduced in any form except as required for the Purpose. Any reproduction of any Confidential Information of the other party by either party shall remain the property of the disclosing party and shall contain confidential or proprietary notices or legends which appear on the original, unless otherwise authorized by the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'reasonably', 'any and all', 'in writing', from this section weakens the control over the dissemination of confidential information. By removing reasonably this affects the part where it states 'Confidential Information shall not be reproduced in any form except as reasonably required for the Purpose.', this also affects the part where it states 'Any reproduction of any Confidential Information of the other party by either party shall remain the property of the disclosing party and shall contain any and all confidential or proprietary notices or legends which appear on the original, unless otherwise authorized in writing by the other party.', which removes explicit requirement for the dissemination to have all confidetial markings.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that it will hold in confidence and not disclose to any third party Confidential Information of the other, except as approved. Each party will use the Confidential Information for the Purpose. Each party will only disclose such information based on the Purpose to its professional advisors, employees and independent contractors.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'reasonably', 'any and all', and 'in writing' from the confidentiality clause weakens the control over confidential information dissemination. The original text limited reproduction to what was 'reasonably required'. By removing 'reasonably', the changed text broadens the scope of permissible reproduction. Furthermore, removing 'any and all' removes the explicit requirement for all confidential markings to be present on reproductions. The removal of 'in writing' removes the requirement that authorization for reproduction be in written form. The Contradicted Text states that each party will hold information in confidence and not disclose to any third party except as approved. The original text provides stronger protection, while the changed text weakens this protection leading to a direct conflict regarding the required level of confidentiality. The weakened control directly contradicts the requirement to hold Confidential Information of the other in confidence.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be terminated by either party at any time upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other party, however its terms shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years from the date of such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be terminated by either party at any time upon notice to the other party, however its terms shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years from the date of such termination.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'thirty (30) days written notice', the text removes critical methods on how one should notify the other party on termination, the type of duration of the termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any notice required or permitted hereunder shall be given in writing and shall be deemed effectively given to the party to be notified: (a) upon personal delivery to the party; (b) one (1) business day following deposit for delivery to the party for delivery within the United States with a nationally recognized overnight courier; (c) for delivery to the party via registered or certified mail, three (3) business days after deposit with the U.S. Post Office for mailing or (d) upon confirmation of facsimile transmission, at the time noted on such confirmation sheet to the party. In all cases, notice shall be made to the party at the location specified on the signature page of this Agreement. Each of the parties to this Agreement may change the location for notice by giving notice to the other party in accordance with the notice provisions contained in this paragraph.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies 'thirty (30) days written notice' for termination. The changed text removes the requirement for 'thirty (30) days written notice', creating a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text' that outlines the acceptable methods for providing notice, including written notice and deemed effective upon specific delivery methods. The removal of the 'thirty (30) days written notice' requirement directly conflicts with the detailed notice procedures outlined in the contradicted text, because written notice will be considered only for a few days.
